description: Unix style pathname pattern expansion using shell-style wildcards.

functions.name: glob

params.name: pathname
params.description: A pathname pattern containing shell-style wildcards: *, ?, and [seq] character ranges.

returns.description: A list of path names matching pathname. The list may be empty if no matches are found.

functions.description: Return a list of paths matching a pathname pattern. The pattern may contain simple shell-style wildcards: * matches everything, ? matches any single character, [seq] matches any character in seq, and [!seq] matches any character not in seq. Does not include filenames starting with a dot unless the pattern also starts with a dot.

functions.name: iglob

params.name: pathname
params.description: A pathname pattern containing shell-style wildcards.

returns.description: An iterator yielding path names matching pathname.

functions.description: Return an iterator which yields the same values as glob() without storing them all simultaneously. Useful for large directories where you want to process matches one at a time.
